title: 8 Interac e-TransferÂ® Reports
body_text:
8	Interac
e-Transfer
Â®
Reports
8.1	Overview of Interac
e-Transfer Reports
Three daily reports and one monthly report are generated either for Interac e-Transfer transactions or for Interac e-Transfer billing purposes:
EMCU (e-Transfer Settlement Summary Report)
EMCD (e-Transfer Detailed Transaction Listing Report)
ECCX (e-Transfer Failed Transactions Report)
Interac e-Transfer Billing Report
Text versions of the reports are available in the financial institutionâs root directory MERG file. PDF versions are available in the financial institutionâs ETFR folder. If there are no transactions for a day, or the report run date is on a weekend or holiday, a report with no transaction details is provided. The deadline for transfers to be included in the reports is 20:59:59 PT. For some financial institutions outside of BC and Ontario, these resports are available via your provincial central.
The Interac e-Transfer Billing report is available on the File and Report exchange. Itâs generated monthly near mid-month. This report provides a breakdown of the fees associated with the amount and specific types of Interac e-Transfer transactions that are created, requested for, sent, and received in a single month by your financial institution.
In addition, financial institutions can subscribe to the Excessive Interac e-Transfers Report. This report can help you detect illegal gambling activities and may also aid in AML compliance. See Section 8.5, Excessive Interac e-Transfers Report, for more information.
A monthly Interac e-Transfer Metrics Report is also available on the File and Report Exchange for financial institutions that offer Interac e-Transfer through Central 1. This report isnât used for settlement, reconciliation, or tracing purposes. It contains information that provides business intelligence on the performance of the service through your financial institution and market comparison on certain metrics (e.g., send and receive transaction volume, average transaction amount, growth rate, and market share).
8.2	EMCU (e-Transfer Settlement Summary Report)
Description
This report lists the total amount that is debited or credited to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: EMCU report
Note: 	The Request Money field displays the totals for Request Money transactions.
Purpose
Use the EMCU report to balance the banking system totals to the amount that was posted to the financial institutionâs account with the appropriate central. See Chapter 5, Interac e-Transfer Settlement and Reconciliation, for more information.
Organization
There is one line which contains a total value of debits and credits to the financial institutionâs account. The total number of debits and credits are listed under the originating debit number and receiving credit number columns. The total credit amount on the EMCU includes the amount of the total Failed Settled Received transactions as reported on the ECCX report.
EMCU â Transaction Details
Column Header
Description
FINANCIAL INSTITUTION NAME
The name of the financial institution
ORIGINATING DEBIT NUMBER
Number of transactions received by the financial institution
ORIGINATING DEBIT AMOUNT
Total amount of transactions received by the financial institution
RECEIVING CREDIT NUMBER
Number of transactions received by the financial institution
RECEIVING CREDIT AMOUNT
Total amount of transactions received by the financial institution
NET SETTLEMENT AMOUNT
Net settlement of Sending Debit Amount + Receiving Credit Amount
E-TRANSFER TYPES
Label for the sub-section showing the details for each type of e-Transfer
TYPE: SEND MONEY
This row shows settlement details for Send Money transaction types.
TYPE: REQUEST MONEY
This row shows settlement details for Request Money transaction types.
Distribution
The report is available daily. If there are no transactions, a report without details is provided.
8.3	EMCD (e-Transfer Detailed Transaction Listing Report)
Description
The e-Transfer Detailed Transaction Listing Report (EMCD) contains all pending transactions, completed transactions, transactions cancelled by the customer, and transactions cancelled by the system. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: EMCD Report
Purpose
Use the EMCD report to balance Interac e-Transfer transactions with the banking system records. See Chapter 5, Interac e-Transfer Settlement and Reconciliation, for more information.
Organization
Transaction Details Section
The transaction details section contains information for each successful Interac e-Transfer transaction. The section is broken into two headings. All successfully received Interac e-Transfer transactions and deposited fulfilled Request Money transactions are listed under the âcredit msgsâ heading. The âdebit msgsâ section lists Interac e-Transfer transactions in the following order: Requested, Completed, Cancelled by Customer, Cancelled by Non-customer, FMR Fulfilled, FMR Completed, FMR Cancelled by Customer, and FMR Cancelled by Non-customer. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: EMCD â Transaction Details
The following table describes each column in the transaction details section:
EMCD â Transaction Details
Column Header
Description
CUSTOMER NAME
The name of the customer sending or receiving funds
DUE DATE
The date that Central 1 receives instructions
for the Interac e-Transfer transaction
REF NO
The Retrieval Reference number
AMOUNT
The amount that was credited or debited from the customerâs account
SETLMNT DATE
The date that settlement occurred (will only show for completed transactions)
ROUTE/TRANSIT
The Routing number of the e-Transfer sending financial institution
INT REF#
The reference number assigned to each transaction by Interac.
Note: This field is case-sensitive.
ACCOUNT
The account number of the customer that originated or received the transaction
SENDING FI NAME\RECEIVING FI NAME
The name of the sending or receiving financial institution
TRANS STATUS
Indicates the Interac e-Transfer transaction status, as follows:
Completed â The Interac
e-Transfer Send Money transaction was successful.
Requested â The Interac e-Transfer Send Money transaction request was sent and is waiting for action from the recipient.
Cancelled by Customer â The Interac e-Transfer Send Money transaction was cancelled (cancellation initiated by the customer).
Cancelled by Non-Customer â The Interac e-Transfer Send Money transaction was cancelled by Interac or Central 1 (on behalf of the financial institution).
FMR Deposited â The customerâs Request was fulfilled by the recipient and deposited into the customerâs account.
FMR Fulfilled â The customer fulfilled a Request that hasnât been deposited yet into the requestorâs account.
FMR Completed â The customer fulfilled a Request that has been deposited into the requestorâs account.
FMR Cancelled by Customer â The customer fulfilled a Request then cancelled it before it was deposited to the requestorâs account.
FMR Cancelled â The customer fulfilled a Request but Interac or Central 1 (on behalf of the financial institution) cancelled it before it was deposited to the requestorâs account.
Summary Section
The Summary section contains totals for each Interac e-Transfer, organized by transaction status. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: EMCD â Summary Section
Distribution
The report is available daily. If there are no transactions, a report without details is provided.
8.4	ECCX (e-Transfer Failed Transactions Report)
Description
The ECCX lists all failed Interac e-Transfer transactions. The deadline for inclusion in the report is 20:59:59 PT.
Purpose
Use the ECCX report to assist with reconciliation processes. In some cases, if a transaction failed, the financial institution may need to manually adjust the debit or credit to its customer. Transactions which may require a manual adjustment have an exclamation mark (!) beside them. See Chapter 5, Interac
e-Transfer Settlement and Reconciliation.
The reference numbers assigned by Interac are between 8 and 16 characters. Reference numbers with 8 to 12 characters apply to regular e-Transfer transactions. Reference numbers with 16 characters apply to Interac e-Transfer for Business transactions. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: ECCX Report
Organization
Transaction Details Section
The transaction details section contains information for each failed Interac e-Transfer transaction. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: ECCX Transaction Details
The following table describes each column in the transaction details section:
ECCX â Transaction Details
Column Header
Description
CUSTOMER NAME
The name of the customer that originated or received the transaction
ORIG/REC FI
For sending e-Transfers: the FI number of the financial institution associated with the account of the customer that initiated the transaction
For receiving e-Transfers: the FI number of the financial institution associated with the customer that received the transaction
ACCOUNT
The account number of the customer that originated or received the transaction
DATE/TIME
The date and timestamp for the transaction from Central 1. The following format is used: YYDDDHHMMSS (Numeric Values only).
AMOUNT
The dollar amount of the transaction that failed
C1 RETRIEVAL REF NO.
The reference number assigned to each transaction by Central 1
INTERAC REF NO.
The reference number assigned to each transaction by Interac
Note: This field is case-sensitive.
TXN TYPE
Indicates the
Interac e-Transfer transaction type, as follows:
Send â Send money transaction
Rec â Receive money transaction
Mcan â Customer Cancel
FMR â Fulfilled Money Request
DMR â Deposit Money Request
CFMR â Customer Cancel Fulfilled Money Request
DR
The response code that is returned in the reply message corresponding to the failed transaction. For a send, the debit response code comes from the bank host or from Central 1. For a receive, the debit response code comes from Interac. See Chapter 9, Interac e-Transfer Response Codes, for more information.
CR
The response code that is returned in the reply message corresponding to the failed transaction. For a send, the credit response code comes from Interac. For a receive, the credit response code comes from the bank host or Central 1. See Chapter 9, Interac e-Transfer Response Codes, for more information.
RDR
The response code that is returned in the reply message corresponding to the failed transaction. For a send, the debit reversal response code comes from the bank host or Central 1. For a receive or customer / customer cancel, the debit reversal response code isnât applicable. See Chapter 9, Interac e-Transfer Response Codes, for more information.
!
Indicator for when manual adjustment to the customer may be required. Financial institution to verify. See Section 5.4, Adjustments for Interac e-Transfer Transactions, for more information.
Summary Section
The Summary section contains totals for each failed Interac e-Transfer transaction type. See Figure 7.
Note:	The Total Failed Settled Receives amount and Total Failed Settled MR Deposits amount are included in the total credits of the EMCU report, which are posted to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account.
Figure 7: ECCX Summary Section
The following table describes each failed transaction type that appears in the ECCX report:
Transaction Type
Description
Total Failed Receives
The total number and amount of all failed receives
Total Failed Settled Receives
The total number of all failed receives that Central 1 settled to the financial institutionâs Central 1 account
Total Failed Sends
The total number of all failed sends
Total Failed Customer Cancels
The total number of all failed customer cancellations
Total Failed Fulfill Money Requests
The total number of all failed fulfilled Request Money transactions
Total Failed Deposit Money Requests
The total number of all failed deposits for Request Money transactions
Total Failed MR Deposits
The total number of all failed but settled deposits for Request Money transactions
Total Failed Customer Cancel Fulfilled MR
The total number of all failed customer cancelled Request Money transactions
Distribution
The report is available daily. If there are no transactions, a report without details is provided.
8.5	Excessive Interac e-Transfers Report
The Excessive Interac e-Transfers Report is available by subscription only. To subscribe, create a ticket in ServiceNow with the following information:
Email address of your fraud/compliance staff who should receive the report. We recommend using a shared inbox or distribution list to ensure continued access.
Defined threshold to categorize âexcessive e-Transfersâ. The default is set at eight per day.
Description
This report identifies customers who receive excessive volumes of Interac e-Transfer transactions. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Excessive e-Transfer Report
Purpose
The purpose of the Excessive Interac e-Transfers Report is to help you detect illegal gambling activities and provide support on AML compliance.
Organization
The email report provides the following information:
Your customerâs account number
The PAN numbers associated to that account number
Total number of Interac e-Transfer transactions
Distribution
The report is provided daily. If there are no excessive Interac e-Transfer transactions to report, the message in the email will state âNo results found.â
8.6	Interac e-Transfer Billing Report
Description
This report provides a detailed breakdown of month-end charges including each transaction type and its associated monthly fees. A single billing entry for the Interac e-Transfer fees is posted to your account via a debit memo. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Interac e-Transfer Billing Report
Note: The report only includes applicable Service Type fees and is based on what activity occurred in the month prior.
Purpose
The purpose of the Interac e-Transfer Billing Report is to summarize your financial institutionâs Interac e-Transfer transactions, related notification activity, and their associated fees.
Organization
The report provides the following information:
Type of Interac services
Transaction fee amounts
Transaction counts
Total transaction fee amounts
Important
: With its new pricing model, Interac now bills for each transaction based on their completion rather than their initiation. Every month Interac provides a report that includes the Transaction Count and this is used to generate the Interac e-Transfer Billing Report. Existing Interac e-Transfer reporting including items counts in Central 1 CAS and CHGS reports based on daily settlement and reconciliation files provide by Interac. There may be differences in the item counts reflected in these reports versus the Interac e-Transfer Billing report due to the different cut-off times and methodology used.
Distribution
The report is accessible through your root directory of the Central 1 File and Report Exchange server. Itâs available monthly and generates around the middle of each month.
END
